title: Santiriopsis trimera

Santiriopsis trimera is a relatively unknown species within the Lauraceae family.

intro: Santiriopsis trimera, also known as Trimera, is a plant species native to Australia and Southeast Asia, belonging to the family Lauraceae. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities, which may help to alleviate symptoms of various diseases such as arthritis and infections. The bioactive constituents of Santiriopsis trimera include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
